info.title: Tuco AI API
info.description: Send iMessages, SMS, and email at scale. Create leads, add to campaigns, get lines, check replies. All endpoints support Bearer API key (`Authorization: Bearer tuco_sk_...`).

post.description: Send one message to an existing lead. Create/import the lead first via `POST /api/leads`, then send using `leadId`. Tuco round-robins lines when `fromLineId` is omitted. Either `message` or `attachmentUrls` (or both) is required.

201.description: Message created and sent or queued

200.description: Message sent using existing duplicate lead

example.duplicateMessage: Lead was duplicate; message sent using existing lead.

400.description: Validation error — see examples for every possible body

value.error: Request body must be a JSON object
Message and attachmentUrls cannot both be empty
Invalid messageType. Must be email, sms, or imessage
No active lines in workspace. Add and activate a line, or provide fromLineId.
Invalid time format. sendWindowStart and sendWindowEnd must be in HH:mm format (e.g., 09:00, 17:00)
Invalid timezone or time window settings. Please check your timezone and time format.
Either recipientEmail or recipientPhone is required (in body or on the lead when leadId is provided).
sendFallbackSmsOnFailed is true but fallback SMS is not configured. Configure fallback in workspace settings (Settings → Fallback) or set sendFallbackSmsOnFailed to false.

403.description: Attachment URL not allowed for this workspace

example.error: Attachment URL does not belong to this organization
Lead not found or access denied

post.description: Create one or more leads. Omit `listId` to use Quick Sends list. Accepts `leads` array or `body` as alias. Returns 200 when all duplicates (existing lead IDs returned).

bulkWithHubSpotContactIds.summary: Multiple leads with HubSpot contact IDs

bulkWithGhlContactIds.summary: Multiple leads with GHL contact IDs

200.description: All leads were duplicates; existing IDs returned

example.message: Lead was a duplicate; existing lead ID returned.

example.error: List not found. The specified listId does not exist or is invalid. Omit listId to use the Quick Sends list.

get.description: List leads with optional `search`, `listId`, `page`, `limit`. Use query `id` for a single lead by ID.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search firstName, lastName, email, phone, companyName (case-insensitive)

200.description: Leads and lists (or single lead when id provided)

400.description: Invalid lead id or no workspace

example.error: Search timed out. Try a more specific search or fewer results.

delete.description: Permanently delete leads from the workspace. List counts and campaign data are updated. Invalid IDs are skipped; deletedCount is the number actually deleted.

invalidId.summary: Invalid lead id in ids array

value.error: Invalid lead id in ids array

post.description: Add an existing lead by `leadId` or create a new lead with `lead` object. Starts the campaign sequence. Returns 200 when lead already in campaign.

newLead.summary: Create new lead and add to campaign

example.message: Lead added to campaign and sequence started
Lead already in campaign (re-entry not allowed).

value.error: Either leadId or lead data is required
Either email or phone is required
Invalid request: must provide leadId or lead data
Failed to process lead through campaign

get.summary: Check replies for a lead (or campaign)
get.description: Returns leads who have replied. Filter by `leadId`, `campaignId`, `recipientEmail`, or `recipientPhone`. Paginated.

parameters.name: campaignId
parameters.description: Replies from leads in this campaign

value.error: Lead not found or access denied

get.description: Returns all lines for the authenticated workspace with usage and health.

get.summary: Check iMessage availability for a single lead
get.description: Check if a specific lead is reachable via iMessage. Updates the lead's availability status. Query param `id` = lead ID.

400.description: Missing id or server not configured

post.summary: Check iMessage availability (single address or bulk by lead IDs)
post.description: Check one phone/email with `address`, or many leads with `leadIds`. Same logic Tuco uses internally.

address.description: Single phone or email to check (no lead required)

leadIds.description: Lead IDs to check in workspace

200.description: Single address: { success, available, address }. Bulk: { success, results, ... }

value.error: Either address or leadIds is required

post.summary: Cancel pending messages for a lead
post.description: Cancel all pending, scheduled, and queued messages for a specific lead. Useful when a lead replies "Stop" or opts out mid-sequence. Accepts `leadId`, `recipientPhone`, or `recipientEmail` — at least one is required. When only a phone or email is provided, Tuco looks up the lead first, then falls back to cancelling by address on messages directly.

byPhone.summary: Cancel by phone (no lead ID needed)

value.error: Either leadId, recipientPhone, or recipientEmail is required

get.description: List all webhooks registered in the current workspace. Secrets are never returned.

post.description: Register a new webhook endpoint. The signing secret is returned **once** in the response — save it immediately. You won't be able to retrieve it again.

201.description: Webhook created. Secret is returned **once** — save it now.

example.name: Production webhook
example.warning: Save this secret now. You will not be able to see it again.

value.error: At least one event must be selected

get.description: Get details for a single webhook. The secret is never returned.

patch.description: Update a webhook's URL, subscribed events, name, description, or active status. Only include the fields you want to change.

value.error: At least one event must be selected

delete.description: Permanently delete a webhook. This stops all future event deliveries to this endpoint.

post.description: Fire a test webhook to your endpoint to verify it's reachable and responding correctly. Sends a real signed POST with `data.test: true` so your handler can distinguish test pings from real events. Uses the webhook's first subscribed event type.

value.error: The operation was aborted due to timeout

bearerAuth.description: Workspace API key: Authorization: Bearer tuco_sk_xxxxxxxxxxxxx

message.description: Text content. Required unless attachmentUrls provided.

contactOwnerEmail.description: Email of the workspace user who owns this contact. Omit to default to the first user in the workspace.
Email of the workspace user who owns this contact. If omitted, invalid, or not a workspace member, defaults to the first user in the workspace.

body.description: Alias for leads (same as leads array)

listId.description: Omit to use Quick Sends list

contactOwnerEmail.description: Default contact owner email for all leads in this batch. Per-lead contactOwnerEmail overrides this.
Email of the assigned contact owner. Null for legacy leads.

message.description: The reply text from the lead (e.g. "Stop")

recentReplies.description: Up to 10 most recent inbound replies from this lead, newest first

parentMessages.description: Up to 10 most recent outbound messages sent to this lead, newest first

leadId.description: Lead ID to cancel messages for

recipientPhone.description: Phone number (E.164) — looks up lead, falls back to matching messages directly

recipientEmail.description: Email address — looks up lead, falls back to matching messages directly

cancelledCount.description: Number of messages cancelled in DB

lastTriggeredAt.description: Last time any event was sent

url.description: HTTPS endpoint URL to receive webhook POSTs

events.description: Event types to subscribe to (at least one required)

secret.description: HMAC signing secret — shown ONCE. Save it now.

statusCode.description: HTTP status code from your endpoint
